title: Flux Reconciliation Health
app: Custom (Flux logs/metrics), Splunk OTel Collector for Kubernetes
spl:
index=devops (sourcetype="fluxcd:controller" OR sourcetype="kube:container_flux")
| search (status="False" AND type="Ready") OR level="error" OR msg="*reconciliation*failed*"
| stats count by namespace, name, kind, message
| sort -count
description: Unhealthy Flux `Kustomization`/`HelmRelease` resources stop automated delivery; monitoring reconciliation ensures continuous GitOps and catches controller or source errors early.
value: Unhealthy Flux `Kustomization`/`HelmRelease` resources stop automated delivery; monitoring reconciliation ensures continuous GitOps and catches controller or source errors early.
grandmaExplanation: Unhealthy Flux / resources stop automated delivery; monitoring reconciliation ensures continuous GitOps and catches controller or source errors early ŌĆö so code changes that fail, leak secrets, or skip checks are caught early.
implementation: Forward Flux source-controller, kustomize-controller, and helm-controller logs (or scrape status conditions from CRDs) into Splunk. Parse Ready=False conditions and error strings. Alert on reconciliation failures or backlog growth. Group by cluster and tenant.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
ŌĆó Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom (Flux logs/metrics), Splunk OTel Collector for Kubernetes.
ŌĆó Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=fluxcd:controller`, `sourcetype=kube:container_flux`.
ŌĆó For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ŌĆö Configure data collection
Forward Flux source-controller, kustomize-controller, and helm-controller logs (or scrape status conditions from CRDs) into Splunk. Parse Ready=False conditions and error strings. Alert on reconciliation failures or backlog growth. Group by cluster and tenant.

Step 2 ŌĆö Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops (sourcetype="fluxcd:controller" OR sourcetype="kube:container_flux")
| search (status="False" AND type="Ready") OR level="error" OR msg="*reconciliation*failed*"
| stats count by namespace, name, kind, message
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Flux Reconciliation Health** ŌĆö Unhealthy Flux `Kustomization`/`HelmRelease` resources stop automated delivery; monitoring reconciliation ensures continuous GitOps and catches controller or source errors early.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=fluxcd:controller`, `sourcetype=kube:container_flux`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Custom (Flux logs/metrics), Splunk OTel Collector for Kubernetes. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feedŌĆörename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: fluxcd:controller, kube:container_flux. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

ŌĆó Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="fluxcd:controller". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
ŌĆó Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
ŌĆó `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by namespace, name, kind, message** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
ŌĆó Orders rows with `sort` ŌĆö combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ŌĆö Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ŌĆö Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed resources), Single value (failed reconciliations), Timeline (controller errors), Bar chart (failures by kind).
visualization: Table (failed resources), Single value (failed reconciliations), Timeline (controller errors), Bar chart (failures by kind).
